_meta.description: Outlier universe for measuring DCF model recalibration impact. These 7 tickers were flagged in the 2026-04-28 launch audit as showing implausibly high MoS values on the Buffett-style screener after PRs #109 + #113. They are NOT in canary_stocks_50.json (which skews large-cap + cyclical). Use this file alongside canary_stocks_50.json — pass via --stocks flag — to validate that DCF audit branches actually compress MoS where the user-visible problem lives, not just where the original canary universe happens to overlap.
_meta.rule: null bound = harness skips that assertion for that stock. Bounds are intentionally null/wide here — the value of this file is in DRIFT measurement (snapshot vs branch), not in gate assertions, since these mid-caps have less reliable reference data than the curated canary 50.
_meta.audit_2026_04_28: All 7 showed MoS in 44-91% band on a 'wide moat / consistent earnings / fair price' preset, which is implausible for liquid names. Goal of any DCF recalibration: compress the displayed MoS toward 10-30%. Track per-ticker delta_pct in canary-diff output and document explanation per CLAUDE.md rule #2.

stocks._note: Already in canary_stocks_50.json — included here for outlier-set drift comparison.
